quarantine.oracle: The parser must produce identical canonical output regardless of the order of dictionary entries in BCF header lines.

evidence.quote: It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

quarantine.mr_name: Permutation of ALT alleles and associated fields
quarantine.oracle: The allele count (AC) and allele frequency (AF) values must remain consistent with the new ALT ordering, and GT field values must be remapped to reflect the new ALT indices while preserving the reference allele (0) semantics.

evidence.quote: AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
AF		    A		    Float		    Allele frequency for each ALT allele in the same order as listed (estimated from primary data, not called genotypes)
LAA		    .			    Integer	    1-based indices into ALT, indicating which alleles are relevant (local) for the current sample
LAA		    .			    Integer	    1-based indices into ALT, indicating which alleles are relevant (local) for the current sample

quarantine.oracle: String values in BCF2 records maintain their type=7 encoding with appropriate length after round-trip, and single characters are represented as strings with length=1

evidence.quote: In BCF2 strings are type=7 with an appropriate length. Single characters are represented as strings with length=1. Type codes 4,6,8–15 are reserved for future use.
In BCF2 strings are type=7 with an appropriate length. Single characters are represented as strings with length=1. Type codes 4,6,8–15 are reserved for future use.
In BCF2 strings are type=7 with an appropriate length. Single characters are represented as strings with length=1. Type codes 4,6,8–15 are reserved for future use.

preconditions: VCF file contains meta-information lines (##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER, etc.)
The ##fileformat line is present and correctly formatted
quarantine.oracle: The biological meaning and semantic interpretation of the VCF file must remain unchanged after reordering the meta-information lines.

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.
It is recommended to include meta-information lines describing the entries used in the body of the VCF file.

[VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.
It is recommended to include meta-information lines describing the entries used in the body of the VCF file.

quarantine.oracle: The parser must produce semantically equivalent output regardless of the order of key-value pairs within structured meta-information lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
